title: Childcare Worker

rationales.taskAutomation: Supervising, comforting, and physically caring for young children is presence-dependent, safety-critical work with essentially no end-to-end automatable share.
rationales.toolMaturity: Baby-monitor cameras and basic learning-app content exist, but no tool substitutes for hands-on child supervision.
rationales.adoption: Adoption of AI in childcare is limited to monitoring cameras and administrative apps; direct care itself remains untouched.
rationales.agenticExposure: Attendance logging, parent-communication updates, and activity planning are agent-ownable slices; supervision and care are not.

salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 39-9011, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Nothing about supervising and comforting a room of toddlers is automatable at scale, so the core job is essentially untouched. Adoption shows up only in the paperwork around it: attendance logs, meal records, and parent updates.
